title: silene alba subsp. eriocalycina
intro: Silene alba subsp. eriocalycina, also known as white campion or white cockle, is a plant species in the carnation family with a delicate appearance and white or pinkish flowers. The medicinal properties of Silene alba subsp. eriocalycina include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, reducing pain and swelling, and fighting off infections. This plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Silene alba subsp. eriocalycina include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
